# servicenow_css_include_relation (Resource)

`servicenow_css_include_relation` manages a relation between a CSS include and a widget dependency within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Associates a CSS include with a widget dependency for load ordering.
resource "servicenow_css_include" "example" {
  name   = "example-css-include"
  source = "url"
  url    = "https://example.com/style.css"
}

resource "servicenow_widget_dependency" "example" {
  name = "example-widget-dependency"
}

resource "servicenow_css_include_relation" "example" {
  css_include_id = servicenow_css_include.example.id
  dependency_id  = servicenow_widget_dependency.example.id
  order          = 100
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `css_include_id` (String) Sys ID of the CSS include to associate with the widget dependency.
- `dependency_id` (String) Sys ID of the widget dependency this CSS include is associated with.

### Optional

- `order` (Number) The load order for the CSS include within the widget dependency.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_css_include_relation.example <sys_id>
```
